rulings.Case Title: PERSOLVE, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DBA VS. CASSANDRA Y PIERCE AKA CASSANDRA V PIERCE, AN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PERSOLVE, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DBA Account Resolution Associates' HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION: Hearing required. Defendant debtor apparently claims that the funds that the Sheriff are holding are exempt social security payments. (See 42 USCA 407; CCP sec. 704.080(c); Kilker v. Stillman (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 320.) A hearing is required and defendant shall provide a copy of her claim for the court to review. Defendant shall also present any proof that she has that the funds are exempt social security payments or proof demonstrating another applicable exemption. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL FRANKLIN VS. MASTEK INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Daniel Franklin'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Of Out-Of-State Witness (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 5/6/2021)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 5, 1- Plaintiff Daniel Franklin's Motion To Quash Subpoena Of Out-Of-State Witness. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. While CCP Sections 2020.220(c) and 2029.300 are inapplicable, the deposition that is subject of this motion was originally noticed on February 18, 2021, past the original discovery cut-off date of January 21, 2021. The Presiding Judge's January 28, 2021 order continuing the trial explicitly stated that "ALL DISCOVERY CLOSED EXCEPT ANY DISCOVERY NOTICED BEFORE THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF MAY PROCEED." As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MARCO BIANCHI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues: Defendant SFUSD's motion for summary adjudication is denied. SFUSD failed to maintain its initial burden of production. (See CCP 437c(p)(2).) Plaintiff's eleventh cause of action alleges that SFUSD was negligent in its "hiring, training and retention" of teachers Tobar and Bianchi. (Fifth Amended Complaint, par. 82 (emphasis added).) SFUSD's moving papers make no argument regarding the training claim and SFUSD presents no evidence regarding its training protocol. Therefore, SFUSD does not cite authority or proffer evidence to show that the eleventh cause of action cannot be established. (See Hawkins v. Wilton (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 936, 945 [burden of production did not shift where moving defendant failed to present evidence negating all theories of liability]; Montague v. AMN Healthcare, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1515, 1524 [examining negligent training claim].) The Court does not consider the new issues raised in SFUSD's reply brief based on due process grounds. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SERGIO GONZALES VS. MARIA LUZ CABRERA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 5, DEFENDANT LS VENTURES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Off calendar. Plaintiff filed an amended complaint after defendant filed this motion for summary judgment. The amended complaint moots the motion. (See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131.) Judge Schulman's March 4, 2021 order granting leave to amend and allowing supplemental briefing on this motion contemplated a May 2021 trial date. The trial was subsequently continued to February 14, 2022. There is therefore no need to immediately hear this motion and it would be procedurally preferable for defendant to file a revised motion that attacks the operative pleading. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: VERA ILLING VS. CAESARS ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 6, DEFENDANT CEASARS HOLDINGS, INC. FKA CEASARS ENTERTAINMENT's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS: Specially appearing Defendant Caesars Entertainment's motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction, the plaintiff must prove, by a preponderance of the evidence, the factual basis justifying the exercise of jurisdiction . . . The plaintiff must do more than merely allege jurisdictional facts; plaintiff must provide affidavits and other authenticated documents demonstrating competent evidence of jurisdictional facts." (BBA Aviation v. Sup. Ct. (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 421, 428-29.) Plaintiff fails to show that Defendant is subject to general jurisdiction in California. General jurisdiction is not available "in every State in which a corporation engages in a substantial, continuous, and systematic course of business." (Daimler AG v. Bauman (2014) 571 U.S. 117, 119.) Rather, the inquiry "is whether that corporation's affiliations with the State are so continuous and systematic as to render it essentially at home in the forum State. (Id. at 137 -139.) Plaintiff fails to show that CHI's affiliations are sufficient to render it essentially at home. Plaintiff's reliance on Defendant's subsidiaries is of no avail. "[F]or the purposes of jurisdiction, the analysis begins with the firm proposition that neither ownership nor control of a subsidiary corporation by a foreign parent corporation, without more, subjects the parent to the jurisdiction of the state where the subsidiary does business." (BBA Aviation v. Sup. Ct. (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th at 430.) Plaintiff fails to show specific jurisdiction. Plaintiff does not show that Defendant purposefully availed itself of California's benefits and that those contacts gave rise to this controversy. (See BBA Aviation, 190 Cal.App.4th at 435.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES ET AL VS. OLIVIER AUFFRET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs? Motion To Strike Defendants' 'General Denial And Affirmative Defenses To Plaintiffs? First Amended Complaint? [Ccp 435 And 436]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 7, 1. PLAINTIFFS LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES, JEAN-YVES LENDORMY's Motion To Strike Defendants' "General Denial And Affirmative Defenses To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint" [CCP 435 And 436]: Plaintiffs' motion to strike is granted with 15 days leave to amend. The "General Denial and Affirmative Defenses to Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint" does not comply with CCP sec. 431.30(g) and CRC 2.112. The Court grants the motion to strike per CCP sec. 436(b) because the pleading is not drawn in conformity with California law. Defendants have leave to amend to clarify precisely what defenses they seek to aver and plead them in compliance with CRC 2.112. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE HOLDEN VS. MAVENTUS GROUP INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Maventus Group Inc. For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 8, DEFENDANT MAVENTUS GROUP INC.'s Motion For Attorneys Fees: Maventus Group, Inc.'s motion for $73,125 in attorney fees and $1,915.91 in costs is granted. Plaintiff's opposition makes three arguments, all unavailing. First, it is asserted that fees should be reduced because the case was "not complex" and the record not "voluminous." This was due in large part to the judicious way in which Maventus counsel defensed plaintiff's case. A total of $73,125 for a year-plus of litigation capped by a case dispositive summary judgment motion is reasonable. Second, plaintiff complains that counsel worked "over 60 hours" on their summary judgment motion. Again, based on my 35 years as a litigator and judge, this was reasonable. (Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49 ("experienced trial judge is the best judge of the value of professional services rendered in his court").) Plaintiff cites an opinion from another jurisdiction that calculated fees per pages of briefing, but such an approach could only encourage prolixity. Third, plaintiff says fees "should be reduced for hours that were block billed." No bills were adduced, so no "block billing" was shown. Defense counsel properly described their services and how many hours they worked in each aspect of the case. (See Syers Properties III, Inc. v. Rankin (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 691, 698-99.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 13, 1. DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., and RASIER, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT; 4 (fraud). Plaintiff fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. 5-6, 8 (assault, battery, intentional infliction of emotional distress). Defendants' demurrer ignores the complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier" and thus liable for assaults "regardless of whether such acts were committed within the course and scope of employment for Uber." (Cmpl., 19:8-17.) Defendants argue that "Uber cannot be held vicariously liable for Doe One's acts, even if he were an employee or agent (which he is not), since he acted on personal motivations unrelated to his duties as an ultrasound technician." (Dem., 8:23-25.) While Defendants' reply shows that some courts have sustained demurrers as to the scope of employment, the cases Defendants rely on are in the teacher/student and priest/parishioner contexts. Defendants do not show that the factual issues in this context can be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint's allegations are sufficiently pled for factual development. This demurrer is overruled. 10 (Ralph Act). The first amended complaint does not adequately plead the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to the demurring defendants, and Plaintiff fails to show on opposition that she can amend to do so. (Cmpl., 20:9-21:5; CACI 3063.) This demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, Llcs Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Linda I. Yen In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 14, 2021, Line 13, 2. DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., and RASIER, LLC's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint: Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. Because the basis for punitive damages is pled with insufficient particularity, that part of the motion is granted with leave to amend. Presumably plaintiff will be amending her complaint to plead fraud with greater particularity, and fraud plays a prominent role in punitive damages law. (See, e.g., CACI 3946.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU) =(302/RBU)
